author: Humanly
text: CORRECTION: strike the 'delivered_in must bound its grep at the current incarnation's creation' subtlety from this topic — it conflated two different git logs. delivered_in greps the DELIVERY log (project repo integration branch), not the balls task log (balls/tasks). The cross-incarnation grep ambiguity is the delivery plugin's concern, not core's, and is resolved by an extension-owned field (delivery writes delivered_in at close.pre) rather than core bounding a grep. Moved to bl-934a. This topic's recency-resolution discipline now applies cleanly to the balls task log only.

author: Delirium
text: Restored from erroneous status=deferred. The github-issues plugin falsely reported the GH issue 'no longer found' (verified OPEN on GitHub) due to a list pagination bug; plugin disabled, bug filed.

author: Unkindest
text:
CONVERGED DESIGN (Unkindest+user dialogue 2026-06-07) — fold this, don't re-derive. Three parts, lead-with-subtraction:

1. RECENCY RESOLUTION (the spine; §2 + §9 + §id-gen). Every id->content lookup = live tasks/<id>.md first, else newest-in-history (git log --diff-filter=D), stop at first hit. Dissolves id-reuse BY CONSTRUCTION (filesystem guarantees <=1 live incarnation), so 'create' stays LIVE-COLLISION-ONLY — no history scan ever; kills the prevent-reuse-scan ball. Same rule resolves dead parent/blocker titles in show/dep-tree.

2. bl ready -> bl list --ready (§9). One listing verb; --ready carries the §10 ready() predicate + its sort; prime's ready-set (data) untouched. Remove the 'ready' verb + its skill/doc references.

3. PAVED PATH TO THE PAST (user: agents treat closed as gone; 'it's in git' is too intimidating even for agents — pave it). bl list gains --all (live+dead) and --closed (dead only). NOT --status closed: status is the derived LIVE ladder (ready/blocked/claimed §3); liveness is an orthogonal SCOPE axis, do not overload status. --closed shows ALL dead (closed UNION dropped); disposition (closed/dropped) is a DERIVED render column from bl-op (§5), not a separate --dropped flag. Dead rows ordered most-recent-down.

CUT (agreed): --grep/date/text filters. That's what pipes are for — listing enumerates, filtering composes downstream. Keep history-query OUT of core list logic.

SUBTLETY to spell out: listing ENUMERATES history entries (a reused id's old dead incarnation is a distinct row) while id-RESOLUTION collapses to newest — listing is over commits, resolution is over ids.

Deliverable = spec edit (§2/§9/§id-gen + §15 RESOLVED revision-log entry, post-freeze pattern like bl-7110/bl-d074/bl-8540). Code (remove ready, add --ready/--all/--closed, history reconstruction) is a SEPARATE build ball.
